# coderd_experimental_agents_model (Resource)

Experimental Coder Agents model configuration. Model configs bind a model identifier to an admin-managed AI provider and configure context, compression, default election, and optional JSON tuning settings. These endpoints are under `/api/experimental` and may change. The server owns default election: set `is_default = true` on at most one model and omit it on others.

## Example Usage

```terraform
// Provider populated from environment variables.
provider "coderd" {}

variable "anthropic_api_key" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}

resource "coderd_experimental_ai_provider" "anthropic" {
  type     = "anthropic"
  name     = "anthropic"
  base_url = "https://api.anthropic.com"

  api_keys = {
    primary = {
      value_wo         = var.anthropic_api_key
      value_wo_version = 1
    }
  }
}

resource "coderd_experimental_agents_model" "sonnet" {
  ai_provider_id = coderd_experimental_ai_provider.anthropic.id
  model          = "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022"
  display_name   = "Claude 3.5 Sonnet"
  enabled        = true
  is_default     = true
  context_limit  = 200000

  # Optional tuning. Omit entirely for server defaults. Write cost values as JSON
  # strings without trailing zeros (e.g. "3", not "3.00") so they round-trip cleanly.
  model_config = jsonencode({
    max_output_tokens = 8192
    temperature       = 0.7
    cost = {
      input_price_per_million_tokens  = "3"
      output_price_per_million_tokens = "15"
    }
  })
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `ai_provider_id` (String) AI provider ID that backs this model. Usually this is `coderd_experimental_ai_provider.<name>.id`. Updating it re-derives the read-only `provider_type` from the referenced provider.
- `context_limit` (Number) Maximum context window for this model. Must be greater than zero.
- `model` (String) Model identifier to use with the referenced provider.

### Optional

- `compression_threshold` (Number) Percentage of the context window at which Coder should compact chat context. Defaults to 70 and must be between 0 and 100.
- `display_name` (String) Display name shown in Coder. Once set, this cannot be cleared to an empty string via the Coder API; omit it to leave the server value unchanged.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether this model configuration is enabled. Defaults to true.
- `is_default` (Boolean) Whether this model is the deployment default. The server guarantees exactly one default: set this to true on at most one model and omit it elsewhere instead of forcing false.
- `model_config` (String) Optional JSON tuning blob for per-call model behavior, such as `max_output_tokens`, `temperature`, `top_p`, `cost`, and `provider_options`. It is passed through to Coder verbatim and compared with JSON semantic equality, so whitespace and key ordering do not matter. Write each value exactly as Coder stores it to avoid a perpetual diff: omit this attribute instead of setting `{}`, and write decimal `cost` values as JSON strings without trailing zeros (for example `"3"`, not `"3.00"`).

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (Number) Creation timestamp as Unix seconds.
- `id` (String) Agents model configuration ID.
- `provider_type` (String) Provider type derived by Coder from `ai_provider_id`, for example `openai`, `anthropic`, or `bedrock`.
- `updated_at` (Number) Last update timestamp as Unix seconds.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by the Agents model configuration UUID returned by Coder.
$ terraform import coderd_experimental_agents_model.sonnet 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
```
Alternatively, in Terraform v1.5.0 and later, an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used:

```terraform
import {
  to = coderd_experimental_agents_model.sonnet
  id = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
}
```
